text:
Agon (アゴン, Agon) was examinee #281 in the 287 th Hunter Exam. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 3, Chapter 22 

Appearance

Agon had bushy eyebrows and a large, crooked nose with hair kept in a ponytail. He wears a two-piece cloak covering most of his body, resembling a dress, and a light long-sleeved top and pants below. In the 1999 anime adaptation, his hair is dark grey and his cloak is lilac, the top worn below matching the color of his cloak, but the pants worn being closer to turquoise or cerulean; in the 2011 anime adaptation, his hair is a lighter shade of grey, and the top and pants worn below his cloak are a matching light-blue. 

Personality

Not much is known about Agon's personality, but he displayed a fearless and prideful attitude when confronted by even the strongest of foes, such as a blood-lust induced Hisoka. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 4, Chapter 28 

Plot

Hunter Exam arc

Agon manages to be one of the 24 Hunter examinees to pass the 3 rd Phase of the Hunter Exam, and make it to the 4 th Phase on Zevil Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 3, Chapter 23 During the 4th Phase of the Hunter Exam, Agon is hunted by Hisoka. Unbeknownst to the two, Gon conceals himself within the forest, intending to steal Hisoka's number plate. As Hisoka closes in on his prey, in an intrepid act of retaliation, Agon pulls out his rapier and taunts his foe. When the two confront one another to attack, Gon uses his fishing pole to steal Hisoka's number plate, catching the two by surprise. Agon dies almost immediately after the confrontation while collapsing to the ground from Hisoka's card attack. 

Abilities & Powers

Agon was a left-handed fencer. 

Battles

Manga Appearances

Translations around the World

References

Trivia

Intertextuality and References

In the first page of Chapter 28, Hisoka chasing Agon through the forest appears to reference the character Fuchi from Junji Ito's Fashion Model in both appearance and style of running.
In the original text, Agon says "!?いいやヒ…ソカ!!!"(fuchi!?iiya hi...hisoka!!!; lit. "Fuchi!? no, Hi-Hisoka!!!"), which was localized to "Freddy? No... Hisoka!!", likely referencing Freddy Krueger instead.
